item.title: Indonesia’s Meals Chief Vows Revamp of Scandal-Tied Agency
item.description: The new head of Indonesia’s free meals agency will refocus the mission of President Prabowo Subianto’s flagship $15 billion program engulfed in a corruption scandal with an eye on budget efficiency and scaled-down plans.

item.title: India Said to Weigh Up to $525 Million Hindustan Zinc Share Sale
item.description: India is considering selling as much as a 2% stake in Hindustan Zinc Ltd. in a transaction that could raise up to 50 billion rupees ($525 million), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nvidia Clears Memory’s Big Three for Vera Rubin HBM4 Supply
item.description: Nvidia Corp. has certified the three biggest memory chipmakers to supply their most advanced high-bandwidth products for the US company’s AI accelerators, Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang confirmed for the first time.

item.title: India Scraps Taxes to Attract Foreign Investment in Bonds
item.description: India scrapped taxes and removed caps on the ownership of some bonds for foreign investors to attract overseas capital and stabilize the rupee, which has been under pressure from higher energy prices and equity market outflows.

item.title: India Central Bank Takes Steps to Boost Rupee, Holds Rate
item.description: India’s central bank announced a raft of measures to support the currency after it plunged to a record low, while it kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged to support the economy.

item.title: Top Chinese Insurers Start Buying Dim Sum Notes Via Bond Connect
item.description: Top Chinese insurers have made their first purchases of yuan-denominated offshore bonds through an expanded southbound investment program, according to people familiar with the matter, capitalizing on changes that encourage more capital into Hong Kong’s market.

item.title: Political Gridlock Saps Romania’s Fight Against Stagflation
item.description: When Mihai Bordeanu, the head of Renault’s Dacia car plant in Romania met with former prime minister Ilie Bolojan last month, he had bad news about the country’s flagship export: sales were dropping and the company needed to lay off workers.

item.title: Mitsubishi UFJ AM Says BOJ May Need Jumbo Rate Hike to Boost Yen
item.description: Mitsubishi UFJ Asset Management said a larger or out-of-cycle Bank of Japan rate hike can’t be ruled out, warning that an expected increase this month may not be enough to prevent further declines in the yen and Japanese government bonds.

item.title: Bitcoin Treasury Firms Shed $62 Billion in Deepening Crypto Rout
item.description: Bitcoin’s slide this week is adding fresh pressure to one of the most ambitious financial experiments to emerge from the recent crypto boom: publicly traded companies created to accumulate digital assets on behalf of investors.

item.title: Shipping’s ‘Biggest Geek’ Charts 40,000-Mile Africa Road Trip
item.description: At nearly 40 years old, Lars Jensen’s Volkswagen camper van appeals to him mostly for its lack of modern features. The vehicle he calls “Sally” is just the right rig to drive around Africa over the next 18 months to explore the world’s most promising supply chain frontier.

item.title: Trump Officials Worry US Loophole Let Chinese Firms Buy Nvidia Blackwell Chips
item.description: Trump officials have spent the past week embroiled in a remarkable argument — over what, exactly, their China tech policy has been for the past year.

item.title: Thailand’s Inflation Unexpectedly Eases, Remains in Target Range
item.description: Thailand’s inflation unexpectedly slowed in May, staying within the central bank’s target range and reducing pressure for an interest-rate response.

item.title: China’s Crowded Solar Industry Pivots to New Areas of Growth
item.description: China’s solar industry is tacitly admitting something it has feared for years: it doesn’t know how to escape chronic overcapacity that has weighed on profits.

item.title: AllianzGI Is Said in Exclusive Talks to Buy UOB Asset Management
item.description: Allianz Global Investors is in exclusive talks to buy the asset management division of Singapore’s United Overseas Bank Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Industrial Metals Swing Lower Ahead of Crucial US Jobs Report
item.description: Copper slumped, and other base metals fell, as caution swept through risk assets ahead of key US employment data that could shape expectations for Federal Reserve policy.

item.title: India Rate-Hike Bets Build as Pricey Oil, Weak Rupee Stoke Inflation Risks
item.description: Most economists expect the RBI to hold today, though an August hike looks near certain.

item.title: China Stock-Yuan Correlation Hits Three-Year High on Optimism
item.description: Chinese stocks and the yuan are moving increasingly in tandem as sentiment toward the country’s assets improves, driven by enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and investors’ push for diversification.

item.title: Iron Ore Futures Head for Fourth Weekly Loss on Seasonal Lull
item.description: Iron ore headed for a fourth straight weekly loss as prices retreat on a seasonally weakening market.

item.title: Ex-Coup Boss Tightens Grip on Guinea With Parliamentary Vote Win
item.description: Guinean President Mamadi Doumbouya has strengthened his hold on power after his party won most of the seats in the May 31 parliamentary elections.

item.title: Amundi Says Asia Tech Rally Faces Fed Risk But No Bubble Yet
item.description: Asia’s AI-led stock boom has room to run further unless a shift in US interest-rate expectations rattles the hyperscalers underpinning the investment cycle, according to Amundi, Europe’s largest asset manager.

item.title: SpaceX Boosts Japan Fundraising Goal to $2.5 Billion in Mega IPO
item.description: SpaceX has increased the amount it’s planning to raise from the Japanese portion of its share sale by a quarter, indicating strong demand among the country’s retail investors.

item.title: Jan de Nul Wins Contract to Dig Argentina’s $10 Billion Waterway
item.description: Belgian dredger Jan de Nul NV and local partner Servimagnus SA won a 25-year contract on Thursday from Argentina’s government to upgrade the nation’s chief trade route in a concession overshadowed by accusations of Chinese influence.

item.title: Indonesia Denies Finance Chief Purbaya to Go as Markets Slump
item.description: Indonesian Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa denied he is resigning from office, after rumors of his departure swept through slumping markets this week.

item.title: Dajin Heavy Drops in Hong Kong Debut After $847 Million Listing
item.description: Shares of wind-power equipment maker Dajin Heavy Industry traded lower in their Hong Kong debut on Friday, following the biggest listing in the city in more than six weeks.

item.title: Philippines to Sell Up to 25B Pesos Treasury Bond Due Nov. 2034
item.description: The Philippines Bureau of the Treasury in Manila said it will sell up to 25 billion pesos ($406.2 million) treasury bonds on June 9.

item.title: Philippine Inflation Slows as Transport Cost Pressures Ease
item.description: Philippine inflation unexpectedly decelerated in May as easing transport costs took pressure off soaring prices triggered by the Iran war.

item.title: Broadcom Is Eschewing Acquisitions in Favor of AI Organic Growth
item.description: Broadcom Inc. Chief Executive Officer Hock Tan, who created the chip behemoth using a series of acquisitions, said the company is now less focused on dealmaking because AI offers stronger growth potential.

item.title: Korea’s Current Account Surplus Stays Near Record on Chip Boom
item.description: South Korea’s current-account surplus remained near record levels in April, underscoring the strength of the country’s semiconductor-driven export boom.

item.title: China Speeds Past Japan in Australian Car Imports on EV Bonanza
item.description: China has surged past Japan to become the biggest car supplier to Australia, with imports from companies such as BYD Co. accelerating to a record high, powered by demand for electric vehicles.

item.title: T&D Will Use Funds From Sale of Insurance Unit for Buybacks
item.description: T&D Holdings Inc. plans to use the roughly ¥188 billion ($1.2 billion) it will get from the sale of a life insurance subsidiary for share buybacks and strategic investments, including in artificial intelligence.
